Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.
  1 other active conversation(s) in this workspace will also be included.
  Use 'entire status' for more information.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	# WP-020 — CMD-V3-02: Assumption Log Primitive
2	
3	**Branch:** `feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log`
4	**Effort:** S — ~4 days
5	**State:** `now`
6	
7	---
8	
9	## Required reading
10	
11	Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.
12	
13	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T9 CMD-V3-02 entry (line 3176), §L Locked Decisions ("assumptions-first" mandate)
14	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
15	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py` — `AssumptionEntry`, `CommandBundle`, `BundleResult` (added by CMD-V3-01 / WP-009)
16	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py` — `apply_bundle()`, `_validate_assumptions()` (added by CMD-V3-01 / WP-009)
17	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — `POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles` route (added by CMD-V3-01)
18	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — element base class; check for existing `agent_trace` field or a mixin pattern
19	- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — API-V3-01 ToolDescriptor for `apply-bundle`
20	
21	Already merged reference — read as patterns, do not re-apply:
22	- CMD-V3-01 (apply-bundle): `AssumptionEntry`, `_validate_assumptions`, `apply_bundle()`, route in `routes_api.py`, ToolDescriptor update in `registry.py`
23	
24	---
25	
26	## Verbatim CMD-V3-02 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3176)
27	
28	**CMD-V3-02 — Assumption log primitive**
29	
30	_Source: §L Locked Decisions ("agent MUST log assumptions JSON before apply"); v2 SKB-08 `AgentAssumptionElem` carried forward as a kernel-level structured field. Reframes from "agent assumption log feature" to "kernel-validated structured field on every bundle."_
31	
32	**Status.** `now`.
33	**Scope.** Every CMD-V3-01 bundle MUST carry a non-empty `assumptions: AssumptionEntry[]`. The kernel does **not** interpret semantics — it does not know whether `confidence: 0.7` is calibrated, does not validate that `source: "brief-line-12"` actually points at the brief. The kernel **only** validates _structure_: required fields, correct types, non-empty array, unique keys.
34	
35	This operationalises §L's "assumptions-first" mandate. The agent fills semantics; the kernel enforces shape. A human script fills the field the same way — the contract is structural, not AI-specific. Every entry's `contestable` flag drives whether the Inspector exposes an "edit + re-run" affordance.
36	
37	**Data model.**
38	```ts
39	type AssumptionEntry = {
40	  key: string;                                  // 'powder-room-area-m2'
41	  value: string | number | boolean;             // 3.0
42	  confidence: number;                           // [0..1]; uncalibrated by the kernel
43	  source: string;                               // 'brief-line-12' | 'sketch-region-3' | 'agent-default'
44	  contestable?: boolean;                        // default true
45	  evidence?: string;                            // optional free-text justification
46	};
47	```
48	**Engine.** Validation in `app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`. Every element the bundle creates gets an `agentTrace` field linking back to the bundle's assumptions array. Activity stream renders assumption entries inline (cross-link to T3). Audit log records every assumption verbatim.
49	**UI.** Inspector for any element with `agentTrace` shows an "Assumption log" section: list of entries; for `contestable: true` entries, an "Edit + re-run" button that opens a small form (key + value), submits a new bundle with the corrected value via CMD-V3-01.
50	**Acceptance.** (a) Bundle without `assumptions` → rejected with `assumption_log_required`. (b) Bundle with malformed entry (missing `confidence`) → rejected with `assumption_log_malformed` + which-entry pointer. (c) Bundle with valid 5-entry log → apply lands; Inspector for the new powder room shows 5 entries; editing "area: 3.0" → "1.8" and clicking re-run submits a new bundle. (d) Activity stream filterable by "Show entries with assumption log" (any caller, not just AI).
51	**Effort.** S — 4 days.
52	**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **CMD-V3-01.** **T3 (activity stream renders assumption entries).** **§L (operational instrument of the locked decision).**
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Setup
57	
58	```bash
59	git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
60	git checkout -b feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log
61	cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
62	python -c "from bim_ai.cmd.types import AssumptionEntry, CommandBundle; print('cmd types ok')"
63	python -c "from bim_ai.cmd.apply_bundle import apply_bundle; print('apply_bundle ok')"
64	uv sync && pnpm install
65	```
66	
67	---
68	
69	## Implementation tasks
70	
71	### 1. `AgentTrace` model — `app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py`
72	
73	Add an `AgentTrace` model that links kernel elements back to the bundle's assumption entries:
74	
75	```python
76	class AgentTrace(BaseModel):
77	    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
78	    bundle_id: str = Field(alias="bundleId")
79	    # Indices into the bundle's assumptions array; empty = all assumptions apply
80	    assumption_keys: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="assumptionKeys")
81	    applied_at: str = Field(alias="appliedAt")  # ISO-8601 UTC timestamp
82	```
83	
84	This model is the payload stored in each element's `agent_trace` field (see task 2).
85	
86	### 2. `agent_trace` field on kernel elements (`elements.py`)
87	
88	Add an optional `agent_trace` field to the base element class (or a shared mixin) so every element can carry provenance:
89	
90	```python
91	agent_trace: "AgentTrace | None" = Field(default=None, alias="agentTrace")
92	```
93	
94	If `elements.py` uses a shared `BaseElement` or `ElementBase` class, add the field there. If not, add it to the most common concrete types (at minimum `WallElem`, `FloorElem`, `RoofElem`, `DoorElem`, `WindowElem`, `StairElem`, `RailingElem`). The field defaults to `None` — all existing elements round-trip unchanged.
95	
96	Import `AgentTrace` at the top of `elements.py` from `bim_ai.cmd.types`; use `TYPE_CHECKING` guard to avoid circular imports if needed.
97	
98	### 3. Attach `agent_trace` in `apply_bundle` (`app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`)
99	
100	After `engine.try_commit_bundle(doc, bundle.commands)` succeeds, iterate over the newly created/modified elements and attach `AgentTrace`:
101	
102	```python
103	from datetime import datetime, timezone
104	
105	def _attach_agent_traces(
106	    new_elements: dict,
107	    old_elements: dict,
108	    bundle: CommandBundle,
109	    bundle_id: str,
110	) -> None:
111	    """Stamp every element that is new or modified by this bundle with an AgentTrace."""
112	    now_iso = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
113	    assumption_keys = [entry.key for entry in bundle.assumptions]
114	    for eid, elem in new_elements.items():
115	        if eid not in old_elements or old_elements[eid] != elem:
116	            # This element was created or modified by the bundle
117	            new_elements[eid] = elem.model_copy(update={
118	                "agent_trace": AgentTrace(
119	                    bundle_id=bundle_id,
120	                    assumption_keys=assumption_keys,
121	                    applied_at=now_iso,
122	                )
123	            })
124	```
125	
126	Call `_attach_agent_traces` only when `mode == "commit"` and `apply_bundle` is returning `applied=True`.
127	
128	The `bundle_id` can be derived as the SHA-256 of the bundle's JSON (consistent with the `checkpoint_snapshot_id` approach in CMD-V3-01) or as a new UUID generated per apply call. Use UUID4 for simplicity unless the WP-009 code already establishes a bundle id convention.
129	
130	### 4. Audit log append (`app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`)
131	
132	When `mode == "commit"` and `applied=True`, write an audit log entry. For v3.0, this is a simple append to a per-model JSONL file:
133	
134	```python
135	def _write_assumption_audit(
136	    model_id: str,
137	    bundle_id: str,
138	    assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry],
139	    applied_at: str,
140	) -> None:
141	    """Append an audit record to <data_dir>/models/<model_id>/audit/assumptions.jsonl.
142	
143	    Creates the directory if it does not exist.
144	    Each line is a JSON object: { bundleId, appliedAt, assumptions: [...] }.
145	    """
146	    import json, os
147	    audit_dir = os.path.join("data", "models", str(model_id), "audit")
148	    os.makedirs(audit_dir, exist_ok=True)
149	    record = {
150	        "bundleId": bundle_id,
151	        "appliedAt": applied_at,
152	        "assumptions": [a.model_dump(by_alias=True) for a in assumptions],
153	    }
154	    with open(os.path.join(audit_dir, "assumptions.jsonl"), "a") as f:
155	        f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
156	```
157	
158	Call `_write_assumption_audit` after `_attach_agent_traces`. This write is a best-effort side effect — wrap in a `try/except` so an I/O failure does not roll back the commit.
159	
160	### 5. TypeScript — `AgentTrace` type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)
161	
162	Add:
163	
164	```ts
165	export type AgentTrace = {
166	  bundleId: string;
167	  assumptionKeys: string[];
168	  appliedAt: string;  // ISO-8601
169	};
170	```
171	
172	Extend the element base type (or each element type) with:
173	
174	```ts
175	agentTrace?: AgentTrace;
176	```
177	
178	### 6. Inspector "Assumption log" section
179	
180	This task is a frontend scaffold — the full Inspector integration belongs to T2 / EDT-V3-01, but this WP ships the React component skeleton so Inspector renders the section when `agentTrace` is present.
181	
182	Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx`:
183	
184	```tsx
185	// Skeleton — full edit+re-run wiring is a follow-on task (T2 / EDT-V3-01)
186	import type { AgentTrace } from '@bim-ai/core';
187	import type { AssumptionEntry } from '../types/cmd';  // adjust import path
188	
189	interface Props {
190	  trace: AgentTrace;
191	  assumptions: AssumptionEntry[];
192	}
193	
194	export function AssumptionLogSection({ trace, assumptions }: Props) {
195	  // Renders assumption entries list; contestable=true entries get an
196	  // "Edit + re-run" button (wired in follow-on T2 WP).
197	  // Uses only CSS tokens — no hex literals.
198	  ...
199	}
200	```
201	
202	The component must use only CSS token variables (`var(--text-sm)`, `var(--color-muted-foreground)`, `var(--ease-paper)`, etc.). No hex literals. No bare `#xxxxxx` in the TSX or any CSS it imports.
203	
204	### 7. Tests
205	
206	- `app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py`:
207	  - `test_agent_trace_attached_on_commit` — after a commit, new elements have `agent_trace.bundle_id` set.
208	  - `test_agent_trace_not_attached_on_dry_run` — dry-run leaves elements without `agent_trace`.
209	  - `test_assumption_keys_match_bundle` — `agent_trace.assumption_keys` equals the bundle's assumption keys.
210	  - `test_audit_log_written_on_commit` — after commit, `data/models/<id>/audit/assumptions.jsonl` exists and contains the bundle record.
211	  - `test_audit_log_not_written_on_dry_run` — dry-run does not write audit log.
212	  - `test_agent_trace_field_is_none_by_default` — constructing any element without trace gives `agent_trace=None`.
213	  - `test_agent_trace_round_trips` — element with `agentTrace` serialises and deserialises correctly.
214	  - `test_bundle_without_assumptions_rejected` — (regression from CMD-V3-01) rejected with `assumption_log_required`.
215	  - `test_bundle_with_malformed_assumptions_rejected` — entry with `confidence: 1.5` rejected with `assumption_log_malformed`.
216	
217	---
218	
219	## Implementation rules
220	
221	- **No AI in any binary.** `apply_bundle` + `_attach_agent_traces` are fully deterministic. No LLM, no inference.
222	- **API-V3-01 contract.** The `AssumptionEntry` shape is already registered in `registry.py` by CMD-V3-01. This WP adds `AgentTrace` to the output schema of the `apply-bundle` ToolDescriptor — update the `outputSchema.elements[].agentTrace` field in `registry.py`.
223	- **Assumptions-first (§L).** The kernel enforces structure; semantics live in the agent. The audit log is append-only — no delete endpoint in v3.
224	- **Backward compatibility.** `agent_trace=None` on existing elements — zero-migration. The `agentTrace` field is optional everywhere.
225	- **Pydantic alias discipline.** All new Python fields use `Field(alias="camelCase")` and `populate_by_name=True`.
226	- **No hex literals in chrome.** `AssumptionLogSection.tsx` must use only CSS token variables.
227	- **Audit log is best-effort.** I/O failures in `_write_assumption_audit` must not abort the commit — always wrap in `try/except Exception`.
228	
229	---
230	
231	## Verify gate
232	
233	```bash
234	pytest -x app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py -v
235	pytest -x app/tests/ -v                               # no regressions
236	pnpm tsc --noEmit
237	pnpm format:check
238	pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
239	```
240	
241	All six acceptance criteria must be demonstrable:
242	(a) Missing assumptions → `assumption_log_required`.
243	(b) Malformed entry → `assumption_log_malformed` + entry index.
244	(c) Valid 5-entry log → elements stamped with `agentTrace`.
245	(d) Audit JSONL exists and contains the record.
246	
247	---
248	
249	## Commit and push
250	
251	```bash
252	git add app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py \
253	        app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py \
254	        app/bim_ai/elements.py \
255	        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
256	        app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py \
257	        packages/core/src/index.ts \
258	        packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
259	git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
260	feat(cmd): CMD-V3-02 assumption log primitive — AgentTrace + audit JSONL + Inspector skeleton
261	
262	AgentTrace model in cmd/types.py; agent_trace field on kernel elements (default None).
263	apply_bundle stamps new/modified elements on commit; _write_assumption_audit appends
264	audit JSONL (best-effort). ToolDescriptor updated with agentTrace in outputSchema.
265	AssumptionLogSection.tsx skeleton (full edit+re-run wiring deferred to T2).
266	
267	Depends on WP-009 (CMD-V3-01).
268	
269	Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
270	EOF
271	)"
272	git push -u origin feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log
273	```
274	
275	---
276	
277	## Final report template
278	
279	Paste into the PR description when merging:
280	
281	```
282	## WP-020 CMD-V3-02 — ship report
283	
284	Branch: feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log  |  PR: <URL>
285	
286	Acceptance criteria
287	- [x] (a) Bundle without assumptions → assumption_log_required
288	- [x] (b) Malformed entry → assumption_log_malformed + entry index
289	- [x] (c) Valid 5-entry log → elements stamped with agentTrace.bundleId
290	- [x] (d) audit/assumptions.jsonl written on commit; not written on dry-run
291	
292	New files: app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py (AgentTrace);
293	           app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py;
294	           packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
295	Modified:  apply_bundle.py, elements.py, api/registry.py, packages/core/src/index.ts
296	
297	Deferred:
298	- Full Inspector edit+re-run wiring (T2 / EDT-V3-01)
299	- Activity stream "Show assumption log" filter (T3 / VER-V3-01)
300	- Centralised audit DB (v3.1; currently JSONL per model)
301	
302	Tracker: §6 T9 CMD-V3-02 → change status to `done`
303	```

# WP-020 — CMD-V3-02: Assumption Log Primitive

**Branch:** `feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log`
**Effort:** S — ~4 days
**State:** `now`

---

## Required reading

Read every file below end-to-end before writing a single line of code.

- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/spec/workpackage-tracker-v3.md` — §6 T9 CMD-V3-02 entry (line 3176), §L Locked Decisions ("assumptions-first" mandate)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/CLAUDE.md`
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py` — `AssumptionEntry`, `CommandBundle`, `BundleResult` (added by CMD-V3-01 / WP-009)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py` — `apply_bundle()`, `_validate_assumptions()` (added by CMD-V3-01 / WP-009)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/routes_api.py` — `POST /api/models/{model_id}/bundles` route (added by CMD-V3-01)
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/elements.py` — element base class; check for existing `agent_trace` field or a mixin pattern
- `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/app/bim_ai/api/registry.py` — API-V3-01 ToolDescriptor for `apply-bundle`

Already merged reference — read as patterns, do not re-apply:
- CMD-V3-01 (apply-bundle): `AssumptionEntry`, `_validate_assumptions`, `apply_bundle()`, route in `routes_api.py`, ToolDescriptor update in `registry.py`

---

## Verbatim CMD-V3-02 tracker entry (§6 T9, line 3176)

**CMD-V3-02 — Assumption log primitive**

_Source: §L Locked Decisions ("agent MUST log assumptions JSON before apply"); v2 SKB-08 `AgentAssumptionElem` carried forward as a kernel-level structured field. Reframes from "agent assumption log feature" to "kernel-validated structured field on every bundle."_

**Status.** `now`.
**Scope.** Every CMD-V3-01 bundle MUST carry a non-empty `assumptions: AssumptionEntry[]`. The kernel does **not** interpret semantics — it does not know whether `confidence: 0.7` is calibrated, does not validate that `source: "brief-line-12"` actually points at the brief. The kernel **only** validates _structure_: required fields, correct types, non-empty array, unique keys.

This operationalises §L's "assumptions-first" mandate. The agent fills semantics; the kernel enforces shape. A human script fills the field the same way — the contract is structural, not AI-specific. Every entry's `contestable` flag drives whether the Inspector exposes an "edit + re-run" affordance.

**Data model.**
```ts
type AssumptionEntry = {
  key: string;                                  // 'powder-room-area-m2'
  value: string | number | boolean;             // 3.0
  confidence: number;                           // [0..1]; uncalibrated by the kernel
  source: string;                               // 'brief-line-12' | 'sketch-region-3' | 'agent-default'
  contestable?: boolean;                        // default true
  evidence?: string;                            // optional free-text justification
};
```
**Engine.** Validation in `app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`. Every element the bundle creates gets an `agentTrace` field linking back to the bundle's assumptions array. Activity stream renders assumption entries inline (cross-link to T3). Audit log records every assumption verbatim.
**UI.** Inspector for any element with `agentTrace` shows an "Assumption log" section: list of entries; for `contestable: true` entries, an "Edit + re-run" button that opens a small form (key + value), submits a new bundle with the corrected value via CMD-V3-01.
**Acceptance.** (a) Bundle without `assumptions` → rejected with `assumption_log_required`. (b) Bundle with malformed entry (missing `confidence`) → rejected with `assumption_log_malformed` + which-entry pointer. (c) Bundle with valid 5-entry log → apply lands; Inspector for the new powder room shows 5 entries; editing "area: 3.0" → "1.8" and clicking re-run submits a new bundle. (d) Activity stream filterable by "Show entries with assumption log" (any caller, not just AI).
**Effort.** S — 4 days.
**Cross-theme references.** **API-V3-01.** **CMD-V3-01.** **T3 (activity stream renders assumption entries).** **§L (operational instrument of the locked decision).**

---

## Setup

```bash
git fetch origin && git checkout main && git pull --ff-only
git checkout -b feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log
cd /Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai
python -c "from bim_ai.cmd.types import AssumptionEntry, CommandBundle; print('cmd types ok')"
python -c "from bim_ai.cmd.apply_bundle import apply_bundle; print('apply_bundle ok')"
uv sync && pnpm install
```

---

## Implementation tasks

### 1. `AgentTrace` model — `app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py`

Add an `AgentTrace` model that links kernel elements back to the bundle's assumption entries:

```python
class AgentTrace(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)
    bundle_id: str = Field(alias="bundleId")
    # Indices into the bundle's assumptions array; empty = all assumptions apply
    assumption_keys: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="assumptionKeys")
    applied_at: str = Field(alias="appliedAt")  # ISO-8601 UTC timestamp
```

This model is the payload stored in each element's `agent_trace` field (see task 2).

### 2. `agent_trace` field on kernel elements (`elements.py`)

Add an optional `agent_trace` field to the base element class (or a shared mixin) so every element can carry provenance:

```python
agent_trace: "AgentTrace | None" = Field(default=None, alias="agentTrace")
```

If `elements.py` uses a shared `BaseElement` or `ElementBase` class, add the field there. If not, add it to the most common concrete types (at minimum `WallElem`, `FloorElem`, `RoofElem`, `DoorElem`, `WindowElem`, `StairElem`, `RailingElem`). The field defaults to `None` — all existing elements round-trip unchanged.

Import `AgentTrace` at the top of `elements.py` from `bim_ai.cmd.types`; use `TYPE_CHECKING` guard to avoid circular imports if needed.

### 3. Attach `agent_trace` in `apply_bundle` (`app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`)

After `engine.try_commit_bundle(doc, bundle.commands)` succeeds, iterate over the newly created/modified elements and attach `AgentTrace`:

```python
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def _attach_agent_traces(
    new_elements: dict,
    old_elements: dict,
    bundle: CommandBundle,
    bundle_id: str,
) -> None:
    """Stamp every element that is new or modified by this bundle with an AgentTrace."""
    now_iso = datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()
    assumption_keys = [entry.key for entry in bundle.assumptions]
    for eid, elem in new_elements.items():
        if eid not in old_elements or old_elements[eid] != elem:
            # This element was created or modified by the bundle
            new_elements[eid] = elem.model_copy(update={
                "agent_trace": AgentTrace(
                    bundle_id=bundle_id,
                    assumption_keys=assumption_keys,
                    applied_at=now_iso,
                )
            })
```

Call `_attach_agent_traces` only when `mode == "commit"` and `apply_bundle` is returning `applied=True`.

The `bundle_id` can be derived as the SHA-256 of the bundle's JSON (consistent with the `checkpoint_snapshot_id` approach in CMD-V3-01) or as a new UUID generated per apply call. Use UUID4 for simplicity unless the WP-009 code already establishes a bundle id convention.

### 4. Audit log append (`app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py`)

When `mode == "commit"` and `applied=True`, write an audit log entry. For v3.0, this is a simple append to a per-model JSONL file:

```python
def _write_assumption_audit(
    model_id: str,
    bundle_id: str,
    assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry],
    applied_at: str,
) -> None:
    """Append an audit record to <data_dir>/models/<model_id>/audit/assumptions.jsonl.

    Creates the directory if it does not exist.
    Each line is a JSON object: { bundleId, appliedAt, assumptions: [...] }.
    """
    import json, os
    audit_dir = os.path.join("data", "models", str(model_id), "audit")
    os.makedirs(audit_dir, exist_ok=True)
    record = {
        "bundleId": bundle_id,
        "appliedAt": applied_at,
        "assumptions": [a.model_dump(by_alias=True) for a in assumptions],
    }
    with open(os.path.join(audit_dir, "assumptions.jsonl"), "a") as f:
        f.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
```

Call `_write_assumption_audit` after `_attach_agent_traces`. This write is a best-effort side effect — wrap in a `try/except` so an I/O failure does not roll back the commit.

### 5. TypeScript — `AgentTrace` type (`packages/core/src/index.ts`)

Add:

```ts
export type AgentTrace = {
  bundleId: string;
  assumptionKeys: string[];
  appliedAt: string;  // ISO-8601
};
```

Extend the element base type (or each element type) with:

```ts
agentTrace?: AgentTrace;
```

### 6. Inspector "Assumption log" section

This task is a frontend scaffold — the full Inspector integration belongs to T2 / EDT-V3-01, but this WP ships the React component skeleton so Inspector renders the section when `agentTrace` is present.

Create `/Users/jhoetter/repos/bim-ai/packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx`:

```tsx
// Skeleton — full edit+re-run wiring is a follow-on task (T2 / EDT-V3-01)
import type { AgentTrace } from '@bim-ai/core';
import type { AssumptionEntry } from '../types/cmd';  // adjust import path

interface Props {
  trace: AgentTrace;
  assumptions: AssumptionEntry[];
}

export function AssumptionLogSection({ trace, assumptions }: Props) {
  // Renders assumption entries list; contestable=true entries get an
  // "Edit + re-run" button (wired in follow-on T2 WP).
  // Uses only CSS tokens — no hex literals.
  ...
}
```

The component must use only CSS token variables (`var(--text-sm)`, `var(--color-muted-foreground)`, `var(--ease-paper)`, etc.). No hex literals. No bare `#xxxxxx` in the TSX or any CSS it imports.

### 7. Tests

- `app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py`:
  - `test_agent_trace_attached_on_commit` — after a commit, new elements have `agent_trace.bundle_id` set.
  - `test_agent_trace_not_attached_on_dry_run` — dry-run leaves elements without `agent_trace`.
  - `test_assumption_keys_match_bundle` — `agent_trace.assumption_keys` equals the bundle's assumption keys.
  - `test_audit_log_written_on_commit` — after commit, `data/models/<id>/audit/assumptions.jsonl` exists and contains the bundle record.
  - `test_audit_log_not_written_on_dry_run` — dry-run does not write audit log.
  - `test_agent_trace_field_is_none_by_default` — constructing any element without trace gives `agent_trace=None`.
  - `test_agent_trace_round_trips` — element with `agentTrace` serialises and deserialises correctly.
  - `test_bundle_without_assumptions_rejected` — (regression from CMD-V3-01) rejected with `assumption_log_required`.
  - `test_bundle_with_malformed_assumptions_rejected` — entry with `confidence: 1.5` rejected with `assumption_log_malformed`.

---

## Implementation rules

- **No AI in any binary.** `apply_bundle` + `_attach_agent_traces` are fully deterministic. No LLM, no inference.
- **API-V3-01 contract.** The `AssumptionEntry` shape is already registered in `registry.py` by CMD-V3-01. This WP adds `AgentTrace` to the output schema of the `apply-bundle` ToolDescriptor — update the `outputSchema.elements[].agentTrace` field in `registry.py`.
- **Assumptions-first (§L).** The kernel enforces structure; semantics live in the agent. The audit log is append-only — no delete endpoint in v3.
- **Backward compatibility.** `agent_trace=None` on existing elements — zero-migration. The `agentTrace` field is optional everywhere.
- **Pydantic alias discipline.** All new Python fields use `Field(alias="camelCase")` and `populate_by_name=True`.
- **No hex literals in chrome.** `AssumptionLogSection.tsx` must use only CSS token variables.
- **Audit log is best-effort.** I/O failures in `_write_assumption_audit` must not abort the commit — always wrap in `try/except Exception`.

---

## Verify gate

```bash
pytest -x app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py -v
pytest -x app/tests/ -v                               # no regressions
pnpm tsc --noEmit
pnpm format:check
pnpm exec eslint packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
```

All six acceptance criteria must be demonstrable:
(a) Missing assumptions → `assumption_log_required`.
(b) Malformed entry → `assumption_log_malformed` + entry index.
(c) Valid 5-entry log → elements stamped with `agentTrace`.
(d) Audit JSONL exists and contains the record.

---

## Commit and push

```bash
git add app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py \
        app/bim_ai/cmd/apply_bundle.py \
        app/bim_ai/elements.py \
        app/bim_ai/api/registry.py \
        app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py \
        packages/core/src/index.ts \
        packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat(cmd): CMD-V3-02 assumption log primitive — AgentTrace + audit JSONL + Inspector skeleton

AgentTrace model in cmd/types.py; agent_trace field on kernel elements (default None).
apply_bundle stamps new/modified elements on commit; _write_assumption_audit appends
audit JSONL (best-effort). ToolDescriptor updated with agentTrace in outputSchema.
AssumptionLogSection.tsx skeleton (full edit+re-run wiring deferred to T2).

Depends on WP-009 (CMD-V3-01).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
git push -u origin feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log
```

---

## Final report template

Paste into the PR description when merging:

```
## WP-020 CMD-V3-02 — ship report

Branch: feat/v3-cmd-v3-02-assumption-log  |  PR: <URL>

Acceptance criteria

- [x] (b) Malformed entry → assumption_log_malformed + entry index
- [x] (c) Valid 5-entry log → elements stamped with agentTrace.bundleId
- [x] (d) audit/assumptions.jsonl written on commit; not written on dry-run

New files: app/bim_ai/cmd/types.py (AgentTrace);
           app/tests/cmd/test_assumption_log.py;
           packages/web/src/inspector/AssumptionLogSection.tsx
Modified:  apply_bundle.py, elements.py, api/registry.py, packages/core/src/index.ts

Deferred:
- Full Inspector edit+re-run wiring (T2 / EDT-V3-01)
- Activity stream "Show assumption log" filter (T3 / VER-V3-01)
- Centralised audit DB (v3.1; currently JSONL per model)

Tracker: §6 T9 CMD-V3-02 → change status to `done`
```

"""CMD-V3-01 — Pydantic types for the command-bundle apply API."""

from __future__ import annotations

from typing import Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field


class AssumptionEntry(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    key: str = Field(min_length=1)
    value: str | int | float | bool
    confidence: float = Field(ge=0, le=1)
    source: str
    contestable: bool = True
    evidence: str | None = None


class ToleranceEntry(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    advisory_class: str = Field(alias="advisoryClass")
    reason: str


class CommandBundle(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    schema_version: Literal["cmd-v3.0"] = Field(default="cmd-v3.0", alias="schemaVersion")
    commands: list[dict[str, Any]]
    assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry] = Field(min_length=1)
    parent_revision: int = Field(alias="parentRevision")
    target_option_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="targetOptionId")
    tolerances: list[ToleranceEntry] | None = None


class BundleResult(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True)

    schema_version: str = Field(default="cmd-v3.0", alias="schemaVersion")
    applied: bool
    new_revision: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="newRevision")
    option_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="optionId")
    violations: list[dict[str, Any]] = Field(default_factory=list)
    checkpoint_snapshot_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="checkpointSnapshotId")

1	"""CMD-V3-01 — Pure apply_bundle function.
2	
3	No DB I/O.  Route layer handles persistence and optimistic-lock enforcement.
4	"""
5	
6	from __future__ import annotations
7	
8	import hashlib
9	import json
10	from typing import Any, Literal
11	
12	from bim_ai.cmd.types import AssumptionEntry, BundleResult, CommandBundle
13	from bim_ai.document import Document
14	from bim_ai.engine import try_commit_bundle
15	
16	
17	def _validate_assumptions(assumptions: list[AssumptionEntry]) -> list[dict[str, Any]]:
18	    """Structural validation — semantics live in the agent."""
19	    advisories: list[dict[str, Any]] = []
20	
21	    if not assumptions:
22	        advisories.append({
23	            "advisoryClass": "assumption_log_required",
24	            "message": "assumptions must be a non-empty array",
25	            "blocking": True,
26	        })
27	        return advisories
28	
29	    seen_keys: set[str] = set()
30	    for i, entry in enumerate(assumptions):
31	        if not entry.key:
32	            advisories.append({
33	                "advisoryClass": "assumption_log_malformed",
34	                "message": f"assumptions[{i}].key must be a non-empty string",
35	                "entryIndex": i,
36	                "blocking": True,
37	            })
38	        if not (0.0 <= entry.confidence <= 1.0):
39	            advisories.append({
40	                "advisoryClass": "assumption_log_malformed",
41	                "message": f"assumptions[{i}].confidence must be in [0, 1], got {entry.confidence}",
42	                "entryIndex": i,
43	                "blocking": True,
44	            })
45	        if entry.key in seen_keys:
46	            advisories.append({
47	                "advisoryClass": "assumption_log_duplicate_key",
48	                "message": f"assumptions[{i}].key '{entry.key}' is duplicated",
49	                "entryIndex": i,
50	                "blocking": True,
51	            })
52	        elif entry.key:
53	            seen_keys.add(entry.key)
54	
55	    return advisories
56	
57	
58	def _checkpoint_id(elements: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
59	    payload = json.dumps(
60	        {k: v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for k, v in elements.items()},
61	        sort_keys=True,
62	    )
63	    return hashlib.sha256(payload.encode()).hexdigest()
64	
65	
66	def apply_bundle(
67	    doc: Document,
68	    bundle: CommandBundle,
69	    mode: Literal["dry_run", "commit"],
70	) -> BundleResult:
71	    """Validate and optionally apply a CommandBundle.
72	
73	    Pure — no DB I/O.  Route layer handles persistence and optimistic-lock.
74	    """
75	    # Step 1: structural assumption validation
76	    assumption_advisories = _validate_assumptions(bundle.assumptions)
77	    if assumption_advisories:
78	        return BundleResult(
79	            applied=False,
80	            violations=assumption_advisories,
81	            checkpoint_snapshot_id=_checkpoint_id(doc.elements),
82	        )
83	
84	    # Step 2: optimistic-concurrency revision guard
85	    if bundle.parent_revision != doc.revision:
86	        return BundleResult(
87	            applied=False,
88	            violations=[{
89	                "advisoryClass": "revision_conflict",
90	                "message": (
91	                    f"parentRevision {bundle.parent_revision} != "
92	                    f"current revision {doc.revision}"
93	                ),
94	                "blocking": True,
95	            }],
96	        )
97	
98	    # Step 3: targetOptionId guard (OPT-V3-01 stubs)
99	    if bundle.target_option_id == "main":
100	        return BundleResult(
101	            applied=False,
102	            violations=[{
103	                "advisoryClass": "direct_main_commit_forbidden",
104	                "message": (
105	                    "targetOptionId 'main' is permanently forbidden; "
106	                    "commits must target a design option"
107	                ),
108	                "blocking": True,
109	            }],
110	        )
111	    if bundle.target_option_id is not None:
112	        # TODO(OPT-V3-01): implement full DesignOption routing (requires KRN-V3-04)
113	        return BundleResult(
114	            applied=False,
115	            violations=[{
116	                "advisoryClass": "option_routing_not_yet_implemented",
117	                "message": (
118	                    f"targetOptionId routing is not yet implemented "
119	                    f"(see OPT-V3-01 / KRN-V3-04); got '{bundle.target_option_id}'"
120	                ),
121	                "blocking": True,
122	            }],
123	        )
124	
125	    # Step 4: run the engine
126	    ok, new_doc, _cmds, violations, _code = try_commit_bundle(doc, bundle.commands)
127	    violations_wire = [v.model_dump(by_alias=True) for v in violations]
128	
129	    # Step 5: checkpoint snapshot id
130	    checkpoint_id = _checkpoint_id(
131	        new_doc.elements if (ok and new_doc is not None) else doc.elements
132	    )
133	
134	    # Step 6: mode branch — dry_run never persists
135	    if mode == "dry_run":
136	        return BundleResult(
137	            applied=False,
138	            violations=violations_wire,
139	            checkpoint_snapshot_id=checkpoint_id,
140	        )
141	
142	    # commit mode
143	    if ok and new_doc is not None:
144	        return BundleResult(
145	            applied=True,
146	            new_revision=new_doc.revision,
147	            violations=violations_wire,
148	            checkpoint_snapshot_id=checkpoint_id,
149	        )
150	    return BundleResult(
151	        applied=False,
152	        violations=violations_wire,
153	        checkpoint_snapshot_id=checkpoint_id,
154	    )
155

222	# Stubs: apply-bundle + model-show (CMD-V3-01 / query stubs)
223	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	
225	register(
226	    ToolDescriptor(
227	        name="apply-bundle",
228	        category="mutation",
229	        inputSchema={
230	            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
231	            "title": "CommandBundleRequest",
232	            "type": "object",
233	            "required": ["bundle"],
234	            "properties": {
235	                "bundle": {
236	                    "type": "object",
237	                    "required": ["schemaVersion", "commands", "assumptions", "parentRevision"],
238	                    "properties": {
239	                        "schemaVersion": {
240	                            "type": "string",
241	                            "enum": ["cmd-v3.0"],
242	                            "description": "Must be 'cmd-v3.0'.",
243	                        },
244	                        "commands": {
245	                            "type": "array",
246	                            "items": {"type": "object"},
247	                            "description": "Array of kernel commands to apply atomically.",
248	                        },
249	                        "assumptions": {
250	                            "type": "array",
251	                            "minItems": 1,
252	                            "items": {
253	                                "type": "object",
254	                                "required": ["key", "value", "confidence", "source"],
255	                                "properties": {
256	                                    "key": {"type": "string", "minLength": 1},
257	                                    "value": {},
258	                                    "confidence": {"type": "number", "minimum": 0, "maximum": 1},
259	                                    "source": {"type": "string"},
260	                                    "contestable": {"type": "boolean", "default": True},
261	                                    "evidence": {"type": "string"},
262	                                },
263	                            },
264	                            "description": "CMD-V3-02 contract — non-empty assumption log.",
265	                        },
266	                        "parentRevision": {
267	                            "type": "integer",
268	                            "description": "Optimistic-concurrency lock: must match current model revision.",
269	                        },
270	                        "targetOptionId": {
271	                            "type": "string",
272	                            "description": "OPT-V3-01: design option target. Absent = current model state.",
273	                        },
274	                        "tolerances": {
275	                            "type": "array",
276	                            "items": {
277	                                "type": "object",
278	                                "required": ["advisoryClass", "reason"],
279	                                "properties": {
280	                                    "advisoryClass": {"type": "string"},
281	                                    "reason": {"type": "string"},
282	                                },
283	                            },
284	                            "description": "Explicit overrides; recorded in audit log (T3 activity stream).",
285	                        },
286	                    },
287	                    "additionalProperties": False,
288	                },
289	                "mode": {
290	                    "type": "string",
291	                    "enum": ["dry_run", "commit"],
292	                    "default": "dry_run",
293	                    "description": "dry_run: validate only; commit: apply if no blocking advisories.",
294	                },
295	                "userId": {
296	                    "type": "string",
297	                    "description": "User identity for undo-stack attribution.",
298	                },
299	            },
300	            "additionalProperties": False,
301	        },
302	        outputSchema={
303	            "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
304	            "title": "BundleResult",
305	            "type": "object",
306	            "required": ["schemaVersion", "applied", "violations"],
307	            "properties": {
308	                "schemaVersion": {"type": "string"},
309	                "applied": {"type": "boolean"},
310	                "newRevision": {"type": "integer"},
311	                "optionId": {"type": "string"},
312	                "violations": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "object"}},
313	                "checkpointSnapshotId": {
314	                    "type": "string",
315	                    "description": "SHA-256 of post-bundle element state; hand-off to VG-V3-01.",
316	                },
317	            },
318	        },
319	        exitCodes={
320	            "ok": ExitCode(code=0, meaning="Bundle applied (commit) or validated (dry-run)"),
321	            "revision_conflict": ExitCode(code=2, meaning="parentRevision does not match current revision"),
322	            "assumption_log_required": ExitCode(code=3, meaning="assumptions field missing or malformed"),
323	            "error": ExitCode(code=1, meaning="Unexpected error"),
324	        },
325	        cliExample=(
326	            "bim-ai apply-bundle bundle.json --base 1 --dry-run\n"
327	            "bim-ai apply-bundle bundle.json --base 1 --commit\n"
328	            "bim-ai apply-bundle bundle.json --base 1 --commit --tolerate constraint_error"
329	        ),
330	        restEndpoint=RestEndpoint(method="POST", path="/api/models/{model_id}/bundles"),
331	        sideEffects="mutates-kernel",
332	        agentSafetyNotes=(
333	            "Default mode is --dry-run; always validate first. "
334	            "parentRevision must equal current model revision or the call is rejected with revision_conflict (HTTP 409). "
335	            "assumptions is required and non-empty (CMD-V3-02 contract). "
336	            "targetOptionId 'main' is permanently forbidden. "
337	            "Same bundle + same parentRevision -> same BundleResult (deterministic)."
338	        ),
339	    )
340	)
341

364	                "elements": {"type": "object"},
365	                "violations": {"type": "array", "items": {"type": "object"}},
366	            },
367	        },

373	        restEndpoint=RestEndpoint(method="GET", path="/api/models/{model_id}/snapshot"),
374	        sideEffects="none",
375	        agentSafetyNotes="Safe to call freely; read-only snapshot.",
376	    )

74	class RoomColorSchemeRow(BaseModel):
75	    """One programme and/or department → fill colour for room-scheme presentation."""
76	
77	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
78	    programme_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="programmeCode")
79	    department: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="department")
80	    scheme_color_hex: str = Field(alias="schemeColorHex")
81	
82	    @field_validator("programme_code", "department", mode="before")
83	    @classmethod
84	    def _strip_optional_str(cls, v: Any) -> str | None:
85	        if v is None:
86	            return None
87	        s = str(v).strip()
88	        return s or None
89	
90	    @field_validator("scheme_color_hex", mode="before")
91	    @classmethod
92	    def _normalize_scheme_hex(cls, v: Any) -> str:
93	        s = str(v).strip()
94	        if not _SCHEME_HEX_PATTERN.fullmatch(s):
95	            raise ValueError("schemeColorHex must be a '#RRGGBB' literal")
96	        return f"#{s[1:].upper()}"
97	
98	    @model_validator(mode="after")
99	    def _needs_programme_or_department(self) -> RoomColorSchemeRow:
100	        if not self.programme_code and not self.department:
101	            raise ValueError("each scheme row needs a non-empty programmeCode and/or department")
102	        return self

146	class MonitorSourceSpec(BaseModel):

149	    See ``packages/core/src/index.ts:MonitorSource`` for the wire shape.

152	      lives in another model; ``None`` for intra-host monitors.
153	    * ``element_id`` is the **source-side** element id (not the prefixed
154	      ``<linkId>::<sourceElemId>`` form).
155	    * ``source_revision_at_copy`` snapshots the source's revision counter at
156	      the moment the copy was made; ``BumpMonitoredRevisions`` re-evaluates
157	      drift against the source's current revision.
158	    * ``drifted`` / ``drifted_fields`` are written by the bump command and
159	      surface as a ``monitored_source_drift`` advisory.
160	    """

163	    link_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="linkId")
164	    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
165	    source_revision_at_copy: int = Field(default=0, alias="sourceRevisionAtCopy")

186	class CurtainPanelOverride(BaseModel):
187	    """KRN-09 — per-cell substitution for a curtain-wall grid cell.
188	
189	    `kind`:
190	      - `empty`      → leave the cell open (no glass, mullions stay)
191	      - `system`     → render a solid panel using the supplied `materialKey`
192	      - `family_instance` → instantiate a custom family at this cell (FAM-01)
193	    """
194	
195	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
196	    kind: CurtainPanelOverrideKind
197	    family_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="familyTypeId")
198	    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
199	
200	
201	def curtain_grid_cell_id(v_index: int, h_index: int) -> str:
202	    """Deterministic cell-id used as the key in `wall.curtainPanelOverrides`."""
203	
204	    return f"v{v_index}h{h_index}"
205	
206	
207	_CELL_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^v(\d+)h(\d+)$")
208	
209	
210	def parse_curtain_grid_cell_id(cell_id: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
211	    """Inverse of `curtain_grid_cell_id`. Raises ValueError on malformed ids."""
212	
213	    m = _CELL_ID_PATTERN.match(cell_id)
214	    if not m:
215	        raise ValueError(
216	            f"curtain panel cell id must match v<col>h<row> (zero-indexed); got '{cell_id}'"
217	        )
218	    return int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
219

651	class StairElem(BaseModel):
652	    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
653	    kind: Literal["stair"] = "stair"
654	    id: str
655	    name: str = "Stair"
656	    base_level_id: str = Field(alias="baseLevelId")
657	    top_level_id: str = Field(alias="topLevelId")
658	    run_start: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runStartMm")
659	    run_end: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runEndMm")
660	    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
661	    riser_mm: float = Field(alias="riserMm", default=175)
662	    tread_mm: float = Field(alias="treadMm", default=275)
663	    # KRN-07 — multi-run support. Defaults preserve the legacy single-run shape.
664	    shape: StairShape = Field(default="straight")
665	    runs: list[StairRun] = Field(default_factory=list)
666	    landings: list[StairLanding] = Field(default_factory=list)
667	    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
668	    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
669	    # KRN-07 closeout — spiral + sketch shape inputs.
670	    center_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="centerMm")
671	    inner_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="innerRadiusMm")
672	    outer_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="outerRadiusMm")
673	    total_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRotationDeg")
674	    sketch_path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sketchPathMm")
675	    # KRN-V3-05 — by_sketch authoring mode fields.
676	    authoring_mode: Literal["by_component", "by_sketch"] = Field(
677	        default="by_component", alias="authoringMode"
678	    )
679	    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="boundaryMm")
680	    tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine] | None = Field(default=None, alias="treadLines")
681	    total_rise_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRiseMm")
682	    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
683	    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
684	    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
685	
686	    @model_validator(mode="after")
687	    def _validate_shape_specific_fields(self) -> StairElem:
688	        if self.authoring_mode == "by_sketch":
689	            if self.boundary_mm is None or len(self.boundary_mm) < 3:
690	                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires boundaryMm with ≥ 3 points")
691	            if self.tread_lines is None or len(self.tread_lines) < 1:
692	                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires treadLines with ≥ 1 entry")
693	            if self.total_rise_mm is None or self.total_rise_mm <= 0:
694	                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires totalRiseMm > 0")
695	            return self
696	        if self.shape == "spiral":
697	            missing = [
698	                name
699	                for name, value in (
700	                    ("centerMm", self.center_mm),
701	                    ("innerRadiusMm", self.inner_radius_mm),
702	                    ("outerRadiusMm", self.outer_radius_mm),
703	                    ("totalRotationDeg", self.total_rotation_deg),
704	                )
705	                if value is None
706	            ]
707	            if missing:
708	                raise ValueError(
709	                    f"spiral stair requires {', '.join(missing)}",
710	                )
711	            if (
712	                self.inner_radius_mm is not None
713	                and self.outer_radius_mm is not None
714	                and self.outer_radius_mm <= self.inner_radius_mm
715	            ):
716	                raise ValueError("spiral stair outerRadiusMm must exceed innerRadiusMm")
717	        elif self.shape == "sketch":
718	            if self.sketch_path_mm is None or len(self.sketch_path_mm) < 2:
719	                raise ValueError("sketch stair requires sketchPathMm with at least two points")
720	        return self
721

from __future__ import annotations

import re
from typing import Annotated, Any, Literal

from pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict, Field, field_validator, model_validator

from bim_ai.roof_geometry import RoofGeometryMode

# --- Geometry primitives ------------------------------------------------------------


class Vec2Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")


class Vec3Mm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    x_mm: float = Field(alias="xMm")
    y_mm: float = Field(alias="yMm")
    z_mm: float = Field(alias="zMm")


class CameraMm(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(populate_by_name=True, extra="ignore")
    position: Vec3Mm
    target: Vec3Mm
    up: Vec3Mm


EvidenceRefKind = Literal["sheet", "viewpoint", "plan_view", "section_cut", "deterministic_png"]


class EvidenceRef(BaseModel):
    """BCF/issue pointer into deterministic evidence rows or PNG basenames."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: EvidenceRefKind
    sheet_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sheetId")
    viewpoint_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="viewpointId")
    plan_view_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="planViewId")
    section_cut_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="sectionCutId")
    png_basename: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="pngBasename")


WallLayerFunction = Literal["structure", "insulation", "finish"]
WallBasisLine = Literal["center", "face_interior", "face_exterior"]
PlanDetailLevelPlan = Literal["coarse", "medium", "fine"]
PhaseFilter = Literal[
    "show_all",
    "show_new_plus_existing",
    "show_demolition_only",
    "show_existing_only",
    "show_new_only",
]

_SCHEME_HEX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^#[0-9A-Fa-f]{6}$")


class ProjectSettingsElem(BaseModel):
    """Singleton-style project datum (canonical units / locale metadata)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["project_settings"] = "project_settings"
    id: str
    length_unit: str = Field(default="millimeter", alias="lengthUnit")
    angular_unit_deg: str = Field(default="degree", alias="angularUnitDeg")

    display_locale: str = Field(default="en-US", alias="displayLocale")


class RoomColorSchemeRow(BaseModel):
    """One programme and/or department → fill colour for room-scheme presentation."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    programme_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="programmeCode")
    department: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="department")
    scheme_color_hex: str = Field(alias="schemeColorHex")

    @field_validator("programme_code", "department", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _strip_optional_str(cls, v: Any) -> str | None:
        if v is None:
            return None
        s = str(v).strip()
        return s or None

    @field_validator("scheme_color_hex", mode="before")
    @classmethod
    def _normalize_scheme_hex(cls, v: Any) -> str:
        s = str(v).strip()
        if not _SCHEME_HEX_PATTERN.fullmatch(s):
            raise ValueError("schemeColorHex must be a '#RRGGBB' literal")
        return f"#{s[1:].upper()}"

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _needs_programme_or_department(self) -> RoomColorSchemeRow:
        if not self.programme_code and not self.department:
            raise ValueError("each scheme row needs a non-empty programmeCode and/or department")
        return self


class RoomColorSchemeElem(BaseModel):
    """Singleton document colour overrides for programme/department fills (replayable deltas)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["room_color_scheme"] = "room_color_scheme"
    id: str
    scheme_rows: list[RoomColorSchemeRow] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="schemeRows")


class WallTypeLayer(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", gt=0)
    layer_function: WallLayerFunction = Field(alias="function")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")


class WallTypeElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["wall_type"] = "wall_type"
    id: str
    name: str = "Wall type"
    layers: list[WallTypeLayer] = Field(default_factory=list)
    basis_line: WallBasisLine = Field(default="center", alias="basisLine")


class FloorTypeElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["floor_type"] = "floor_type"
    id: str
    name: str = "Floor type"
    layers: list[WallTypeLayer] = Field(default_factory=list)


class RoofTypeElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["roof_type"] = "roof_type"
    id: str
    name: str = "Roof type"
    layers: list[WallTypeLayer] = Field(default_factory=list)


class MonitorSourceSpec(BaseModel):
    """FED-03 — structured Copy/Monitor source pointer.

    See ``packages/core/src/index.ts:MonitorSource`` for the wire shape.

    * ``link_id`` is the host's ``link_model`` element id when the source
      lives in another model; ``None`` for intra-host monitors.
    * ``element_id`` is the **source-side** element id (not the prefixed
      ``<linkId>::<sourceElemId>`` form).
    * ``source_revision_at_copy`` snapshots the source's revision counter at
      the moment the copy was made; ``BumpMonitoredRevisions`` re-evaluates
      drift against the source's current revision.
    * ``drifted`` / ``drifted_fields`` are written by the bump command and
      surface as a ``monitored_source_drift`` advisory.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    link_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="linkId")
    element_id: str = Field(alias="elementId")
    source_revision_at_copy: int = Field(default=0, alias="sourceRevisionAtCopy")
    drifted: bool = Field(default=False)
    drifted_fields: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="driftedFields")


class LevelElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["level"] = "level"
    id: str
    name: str = "Untitled Level"
    elevation_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="elevationMm")
    datum_kind: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="datumKind")
    parent_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="parentLevelId")
    offset_from_parent_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="offsetFromParentMm")
    monitor_source: MonitorSourceSpec | None = Field(default=None, alias="monitorSource")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)


CurtainPanelOverrideKind = Literal["empty", "system", "family_instance"]


class CurtainPanelOverride(BaseModel):
    """KRN-09 — per-cell substitution for a curtain-wall grid cell.

    `kind`:
      - `empty`      → leave the cell open (no glass, mullions stay)
      - `system`     → render a solid panel using the supplied `materialKey`
      - `family_instance` → instantiate a custom family at this cell (FAM-01)
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: CurtainPanelOverrideKind
    family_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="familyTypeId")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")


def curtain_grid_cell_id(v_index: int, h_index: int) -> str:
    """Deterministic cell-id used as the key in `wall.curtainPanelOverrides`."""

    return f"v{v_index}h{h_index}"


_CELL_ID_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^v(\d+)h(\d+)$")


def parse_curtain_grid_cell_id(cell_id: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    """Inverse of `curtain_grid_cell_id`. Raises ValueError on malformed ids."""

    m = _CELL_ID_PATTERN.match(cell_id)
    if not m:
        raise ValueError(
            f"curtain panel cell id must match v<col>h<row> (zero-indexed); got '{cell_id}'"
        )
    return int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))


class WallRecessZone(BaseModel):
    """KRN-16 — wall recess / setback zone along the wall's alongT axis."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    along_t_start: float = Field(alias="alongTStart", ge=0, le=1)
    along_t_end: float = Field(alias="alongTEnd", ge=0, le=1)
    setback_mm: float = Field(alias="setbackMm", gt=0)
    sill_height_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="sillHeightMm", ge=0)
    head_height_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="headHeightMm", ge=0)
    floor_continues: bool = Field(default=False, alias="floorContinues")


class WallStackComponent(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    wall_type_id: str = Field(alias="wallTypeId")
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", gt=0)


class WallStack(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    components: list[WallStackComponent] = Field(default_factory=list)


class WallElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["wall"] = "wall"
    id: str
    name: str = "Wall"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    start: Vec2Mm
    end: Vec2Mm
    thickness_mm: float = Field(alias="thicknessMm", default=200)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800)
    wall_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="wallTypeId")
    base_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="baseConstraintLevelId")
    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
    roof_attachment_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="roofAttachmentId")
    insulation_extension_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="insulationExtensionMm")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    # IFC-04: optional OmniClass / Uniclass / NSCC code; emitted via
    # IfcClassificationReference when set.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    is_curtain_wall: bool = Field(default=False, alias="isCurtainWall")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_id: str | None = Field(
        default=None,
        alias="phaseId",
        description="SKB-08 phase tag carried forward when materialised from a mass.",
    )
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
    curtain_wall_v_count: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="curtainWallVCount")
    curtain_wall_h_count: int | None = Field(default=None, alias="curtainWallHCount")
    curtain_panel_overrides: dict[str, CurtainPanelOverride] | None = Field(
        default=None, alias="curtainPanelOverrides"
    )
    recess_zones: list[WallRecessZone] | None = Field(default=None, alias="recessZones")
    stack: WallStack | None = Field(default=None)


DoorOperationType = Literal[
    "swing_single",
    "swing_double",
    "sliding_single",
    "sliding_double",
    "bi_fold",
    "pocket",
    "pivot",
    "automatic_double",
]

DoorSlidingTrackSide = Literal["wall_face", "in_pocket"]


class DoorElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["door"] = "door"
    id: str
    name: str = "Door"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    along_t: float = Field(alias="alongT", ge=0, le=1)
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=900)
    family_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="familyTypeId")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    host_cut_depth_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="hostCutDepthMm")
    reveal_interior_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="revealInteriorMm")
    interlock_grade: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="interlockGrade")
    lod_plan: Literal["simple", "detailed"] | None = Field(default=None, alias="lodPlan")
    operation_type: DoorOperationType | None = Field(default=None, alias="operationType")
    sliding_track_side: DoorSlidingTrackSide | None = Field(default=None, alias="slidingTrackSide")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


WindowOutlineKind = Literal[
    "rectangle",
    "arched_top",
    "gable_trapezoid",
    "circle",
    "octagon",
    "custom",
]


class WindowElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["window"] = "window"
    id: str
    name: str = "Window"
    wall_id: str = Field(alias="wallId")
    along_t: float = Field(alias="alongT", ge=0, le=1)
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1200)
    sill_height_mm: float = Field(alias="sillHeightMm", default=900)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=1500)
    family_type_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="familyTypeId")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    host_cut_depth_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="hostCutDepthMm")
    reveal_interior_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="revealInteriorMm")
    interlock_grade: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="interlockGrade")
    seal_rebate_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="sealRebateMm")
    lod_plan: Literal["simple", "detailed"] | None = Field(default=None, alias="lodPlan")
    outline_kind: WindowOutlineKind | None = Field(default=None, alias="outlineKind")
    outline_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="outlineMm")
    attached_roof_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="attachedRoofId")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")


class WallOpeningElem(BaseModel):
    """Frameless rectangular cut in a host wall (no door / window family)."""

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["wall_opening"] = "wall_opening"
    id: str
    name: str = "Wall opening"
    host_wall_id: str = Field(alias="hostWallId")
    along_t_start: float = Field(alias="alongTStart", ge=0, le=1)
    along_t_end: float = Field(alias="alongTEnd", ge=0, le=1)
    sill_height_mm: float = Field(alias="sillHeightMm", ge=0)
    head_height_mm: float = Field(alias="headHeightMm", ge=0)
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _check_bounds(self) -> WallOpeningElem:
        if self.along_t_start >= self.along_t_end:
            raise ValueError("wall_opening alongTStart must be < alongTEnd")
        if self.head_height_mm <= self.sill_height_mm:
            raise ValueError("wall_opening headHeightMm must be > sillHeightMm")
        return self


class RoomElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)

class ReferencePlaneElem(BaseModel):

class ViewpointElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["viewpoint"] = "viewpoint"
    id: str
    name: str = "View"
    camera: CameraMm
    mode: Literal["plan_2d", "orbit_3d", "plan_canvas"] = "orbit_3d"

class StairElem(BaseModel):
    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["stair"] = "stair"
    id: str
    name: str = "Stair"
    base_level_id: str = Field(alias="baseLevelId")
    top_level_id: str = Field(alias="topLevelId")
    run_start: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runStartMm")
    run_end: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="runEndMm")
    width_mm: float = Field(alias="widthMm", default=1000)
    riser_mm: float = Field(alias="riserMm", default=175)
    tread_mm: float = Field(alias="treadMm", default=275)
    # KRN-07 — multi-run support. Defaults preserve the legacy single-run shape.
    shape: StairShape = Field(default="straight")
    runs: list[StairRun] = Field(default_factory=list)
    landings: list[StairLanding] = Field(default_factory=list)
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    # KRN-07 closeout — spiral + sketch shape inputs.
    center_mm: Vec2Mm | None = Field(default=None, alias="centerMm")
    inner_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="innerRadiusMm")
    outer_radius_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="outerRadiusMm")
    total_rotation_deg: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRotationDeg")
    sketch_path_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="sketchPathMm")
    # KRN-V3-05 — by_sketch authoring mode fields.
    authoring_mode: Literal["by_component", "by_sketch"] = Field(
        default="by_component", alias="authoringMode"
    )
    boundary_mm: list[Vec2Mm] | None = Field(default=None, alias="boundaryMm")
    tread_lines: list[StairTreadLine] | None = Field(default=None, alias="treadLines")
    total_rise_mm: float | None = Field(default=None, alias="totalRiseMm")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _validate_shape_specific_fields(self) -> StairElem:
        if self.authoring_mode == "by_sketch":
            if self.boundary_mm is None or len(self.boundary_mm) < 3:
                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires boundaryMm with ≥ 3 points")
            if self.tread_lines is None or len(self.tread_lines) < 1:
                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires treadLines with ≥ 1 entry")
            if self.total_rise_mm is None or self.total_rise_mm <= 0:
                raise ValueError("by_sketch stair requires totalRiseMm > 0")
            return self
        if self.shape == "spiral":
            missing = [
                name
                for name, value in (
                    ("centerMm", self.center_mm),
                    ("innerRadiusMm", self.inner_radius_mm),
                    ("outerRadiusMm", self.outer_radius_mm),
                    ("totalRotationDeg", self.total_rotation_deg),
                )
                if value is None
            ]
            if missing:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"spiral stair requires {', '.join(missing)}",
                )
            if (
                self.inner_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm is not None
                and self.outer_radius_mm <= self.inner_radius_mm
            ):
                raise ValueError("spiral stair outerRadiusMm must exceed innerRadiusMm")
        elif self.shape == "sketch":
            if self.sketch_path_mm is None or len(self.sketch_path_mm) < 2:
                raise ValueError("sketch stair requires sketchPathMm with at least two points")
        return self

class SelectionSetRuleSpec(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a single rule in a selection set's filter list.

    ``link_scope`` controls which models the rule resolves against:

    * ``'host'`` (default) — only host elements match.
    * ``'all_links'`` — every ``link_model`` element is walked; matching source
      elements are included with their AABBs transformed by the link's
      ``positionMm`` + ``rotationDeg``.
    * ``{ 'specificLinkId': '<link-id>' }`` — restrict matches to a single
      link.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    field: Literal["category", "level", "typeName"]
    operator: Literal["equals", "contains"]
    value: str
    link_scope: str | dict[str, str] | None = Field(default=None, alias="linkScope")


class SelectionSetElem(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a named filter that resolves to a list of element ids.

    Stored in the model so it can be referenced by clash tests and (later)
    schedules. The element itself is non-graphical.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["selection_set"] = "selection_set"
    id: str
    name: str = "Selection Set"
    filter_rules: list[SelectionSetRuleSpec] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="filterRules")


class ClashResultSpec(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a single pair-wise clash between two resolved elements.

    ``link_chain_a`` / ``link_chain_b`` are empty arrays for host elements
    and ``[link_id]`` for elements pulled from a linked model. Multi-hop
    transitive links are deferred (FED-01's expander is single-hop only).
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    element_id_a: str = Field(alias="elementIdA")
    element_id_b: str = Field(alias="elementIdB")
    distance_mm: float = Field(alias="distanceMm")
    link_chain_a: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="linkChainA")
    link_chain_b: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="linkChainB")


class ClashTestElem(BaseModel):
    """FED-02: a pair of selection sets that the engine clash-tests on demand.

    ``set_a_ids`` / ``set_b_ids`` are lists of ``selection_set`` element ids
    (multiple sets are unioned). Clash detection is run by the
    ``RunClashTest`` command which writes its findings into ``results``.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["clash_test"] = "clash_test"
    id: str
    name: str = "Clash Test"
    set_a_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="setAIds")
    set_b_ids: list[str] = Field(default_factory=list, alias="setBIds")
    tolerance_mm: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="toleranceMm")
    results: list[ClashResultSpec] | None = None

class ColumnElem(BaseModel):
    """EDT-04 — vertical structural column placed at a single point on a level.

    Cross-section is a rectangle (bMm × hMm) with optional rotation about
    the vertical axis. Spans from the host level upward by ``heightMm``;
    optional top constraint mirrors the wall datum-constraint pattern.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="ignore", populate_by_name=True)
    kind: Literal["column"] = "column"
    id: str
    name: str = "Column"
    level_id: str = Field(alias="levelId")
    position_mm: Vec2Mm = Field(alias="positionMm")
    b_mm: float = Field(alias="bMm", default=300, gt=0)
    h_mm: float = Field(alias="hMm", default=300, gt=0)
    height_mm: float = Field(alias="heightMm", default=2800, gt=0)
    rotation_deg: float = Field(default=0.0, alias="rotationDeg")
    material_key: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="materialKey")
    base_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="baseConstraintOffsetMm")
    top_constraint_level_id: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="topConstraintLevelId")
    top_constraint_offset_mm: float = Field(default=0, alias="topConstraintOffsetMm")
    # IFC-04: optional classification code emitted as IfcClassificationReference.
    ifc_classification_code: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="ifcClassificationCode")
    pinned: bool = Field(default=False)
    phase_created: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseCreated")
    phase_demolished: str | None = Field(default=None, alias="phaseDemolished")
